features.title: Human Confirmation API
features.description: Implement the human confirmation REST API for the Web Collaboration Beta (F2-06). Adds the two documented endpoints in docs/specs/interface_spec.md § 6: GET /confirmations?status=pending (list accessible confirmations) and POST /confirmations/:id/decide (approve or reject a pending confirmation). Reuses the F2-02 actor helper, the F-003 canDecideConfirmation authorization helper, the F-002 state-machine module (assertTransition + human_confirm / human_reject triggers), the F2-05 card repository (getCardContext, updateCardState, insertStateTransition), and the F-004 runWithAudit transaction wrapper. Every decision write persists one audit_entries row (entity_type 'confirm'), one realtime_events row (resource_type 'confirmation'), one state_transitions row (when the card state changes), and the confirmation status update inside a single transaction; failure rolls back the decision. Pending human confirmations block the documented unblock / complete mutations via the F-003 gate, but this change does not introduce new gating logic.
acceptance_criteria: GET /api/v1/confirmations?status=pending returns 200 with {items, total}; initiator sees all pending confirmations on cards in their goal spaces; chain_user / viewer see only pending confirmations on cards they can read; defaults to status=pending; accepts status=approved|rejected|cancelled; 400 INVALID_FIELD for out-of-range status; 401 without session.

POST /api/v1/confirmations/:id/decide returns 200 with DecideConfirmationResponse; initiator only; 403 for non-initiator; 404 for missing confirmation; 409 STATE_CONFLICT for already-decided or cancelled confirmation; 422 VALIDATION_ERROR for invalid outcome or missing reason on reject; 401 without session.

Approval path: when target_state is present, the card transitions to target_state via 'human_confirm' trigger; state_transitions row written with actor='human'; returns card_state_changed=true, new_card_state=target_state.

Rejection path: the card transitions to 'blocked' via 'human_reject' trigger; state_transitions row written with actor='human'; decision_reason records the reject reason; returns card_state_changed=true, new_card_state='blocked'.

Every decision write persists exactly one audit_entries row (entity_type 'confirm') and one realtime_events row (resource_type 'confirmation') inside a single transaction; failure rolls back the decision.
Every state-changing decision persists exactly one state_transitions row inside the same transaction (F-002 contract).
apps/web/src/lib/api/actor.ts is used by every F2-06 route (no F2-02 / F2-03 / F2-04 / F2-05 helper duplication).
canDecideConfirmation is used by the decide service; the helper already encodes the § 5 mandatory gate (returns false when status is not 'pending' or actor is not the goal-space initiator).
assertTransition is used for both the approve and reject paths; IllegalTransitionError is mapped to 409 STATE_CONFLICT.

HUMAN_CONFIRMATION_REALTIME_EVENTS + HUMAN_CONFIRMATION_AUDIT_ENTITY_TYPE are exported from apps/web/src/lib/services/confirmations.ts as a single source of truth for F2-08 SSE filtering; a snapshot test pins them.
pnpm --filter @keplar/web test passes (the full web suite stays green; F2-05's 485 tests remain green).
pnpm check passes (typecheck + lint + test + build + format:check) with environment warnings only.
No files outside the F2-06 file set or unrelated prior changes are modified.
test_needs: Write failing confirmation API contract tests first (list + decide, plus auth matrix, plus already-decided guard)
Cover membership matrix: initiator (sees all), chain_user (filtered by card access), viewer (filtered by card access)
Cover approve + reject paths with target_state validation
Cover audit_entries + realtime_events + state_transitions write paths per decision
Cover realtime event constant snapshot for F2-08 handoff
Run full web suite for regression
definition_of_done: Required verification matrix items pass or have documented exceptions
No unresolved blocking review findings remain
Handoff document written for F2-07 / F2-08
